texto: The notion that I had walked twelve hundred miles since Rotterdam filled me with a legitimate feeling of something achieved. But why should the thought that nobody knew where I was, as though I were in flight from bloodhounds or from worshipping corybants bent on dismemberment, generate such a feeling of triumph? It always did.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Patrick Leigh Fermor que provoca temas ligados a sucesso. Ao questionar “The notion that I had walked twelve hundred miles since Rotterdam filled me with a legitimate fee…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sucesso, independence, walk, com foco em termino e walk.
